Editorial Design Publishing Long-form Content Print & Digital

Editorial Design
& Publishing System

A comprehensive editorial design system for Harappa Publications — translating long-form content across books, courses, and editorial publications into structured, readable narratives, supported by a consistent visual and illustration language across print and digital formats.

Client
Harappa Publications
upGrad Enterprise
Deliverable
Editorial System
Books, Courses, Digital
Year
2022
~60 hours
Tools
InDesign · Photoshop
Illustrator
Editorial Design & Publishing System
Harappa Publications, 2022
Case Study
Context & Challenge

One organisation, many formats — no system.

Harappa Publications was producing content across a wide range of formats — print books, course materials, editorial publications, and digital content — each with different structural requirements, production constraints, and audiences. The volume was high and growing, but the visual and editorial approach was inconsistent across output.

Without a unified editorial system, long-form content was difficult to navigate, visual quality varied between formats, and production was inefficient. The challenge was to build a scalable editorial design framework that could carry Harappa's narrative quality consistently across every format it published in — from a hardcover book to a digital learning module — while integrating the brand's distinctive mixed-media illustration language.

Objective

Readability and brand, at publishing scale.

To design a scalable editorial and publishing system that balanced readability and visual engagement across formats — establishing consistent information hierarchy, layout logic, typographic system, and illustration integration rules that could be applied efficiently across a high-volume content production environment. The system needed to make every published piece feel part of the same editorial world, regardless of format or medium.

My Role
  • Designed editorial layouts and information hierarchy for books, course materials, and editorial publications across print and digital formats
  • Built the publishing system — grid structure, typographic rules, chapter and section logic, image integration principles
  • Structured long-form content for clarity and navigability, adapting prose-heavy manuscripts into designed, readable formats
  • Integrated custom mixed-media illustrations into the editorial system, ensuring visual coherence between text and image across layouts
  • Adapted layouts for print production specifications and digital delivery requirements across Harappa's content formats
  • Produced a marketing video for a book published to coincide with India's 75th Independence Day
Adobe InDesign Photoshop Illustrator Print Production
Impact

A content ecosystem, made coherent.

The editorial system enabled consistent, efficient production across Harappa's published output — improving clarity and navigability across books and courses, establishing a recognisable visual editorial language, and making high-quality content design repeatable at volume. The system became the foundation for ongoing publishing activity across Harappa and upGrad Enterprise's content initiatives.

75 Thrive Skills — the complete publication series. Each book cover demonstrates the typographic and color system consistently applied across the full publication ecosystem, with the yellow brand foundation and systematic numbering creating immediate series recognition.

Interior spread — "Powerful Storytelling" chapter layout demonstrating the grid system, typographic hierarchy, and mixed-media illustration integration. The editorial framework balances dense content with visual breathing room and clear navigation.

Framework overview — the complete "Wisdom That Inspires Us" spread showing how the Thrive Skills taxonomy (Think, Solve, Communicate, Collaborate, Lead) is systematically presented within the editorial grid system.

"Think" spread — demonstrating the editorial system's approach to mixed-media collage integration. Greyscale portrait photography combined with yellow geometric elements and comprehensive skill listings, maintaining visual hierarchy across content-dense layouts.

Motivational typography — "Do One Thing Every Day That Grows You" spread demonstrating how the editorial system handles large-scale type treatment while maintaining the established grid logic and brand color integration.

Chapter opener — "Attention" section showing the system's approach to minimalist page layouts. The hand-pulling illustration demonstrates how custom artwork integrates within the editorial framework while maintaining visual consistency.

System flexibility — the editorial framework applied to different content types and publication formats, showing how the grid system, typography, and color logic scale across varying content densities and layout requirements.

Illustration integration — close view of how custom mixed-media illustrations and collage elements are systematically integrated within the editorial grid, creating visual interest while maintaining readability and information hierarchy.

Marketing video — frame from the promotional video created to support the 75 Thrive Skills publication launch. The project extended beyond editorial design to include motion graphics and marketing collateral, demonstrating the system's adaptability across media formats.

Cover design — the complete book cover demonstrating how the editorial system's typographic and color logic extends to external presentation. The embossed "GROWS" treatment and systematic numbering create a recognizable publication series that works both individually and as a collection.

Editorial design at its best is invisible — the reader moves through content without noticing the system holding it together.

— Design approach, Editorial Design & Publishing System, Harappa Publications, 2022

Process
01
Content Audit & System Logic

Reviewing Harappa's existing published output across formats — identifying structural inconsistencies, understanding the range of content types in production, and establishing the system logic the editorial framework needed to address. Determining what was shared across all formats versus what needed format-specific rules.

02
Grid & Typography

Building the core editorial infrastructure — the grid system, typographic hierarchy, and spacing logic that would govern how content sat on the page across print and digital contexts. Designing for dense long-form text as the primary use case, with clear navigation structures for chapter openers, pull quotes, captions, and callout elements.

03
Illustration Integration

Developing the rules for how Harappa's mixed-media illustrations were integrated into editorial layouts — scale, placement, bleed treatment, relationship to text columns. Ensuring the illustration language and the editorial system reinforced each other rather than competing, and that the integration logic could be applied consistently by production teams.

04
Format Adaptation & Production

Adapting the editorial system across formats — print book production, course materials, digital editorial content, and the Independence Day publication — each requiring specific production preparation. Producing the marketing video for the Independence Day book launch, extending the project's scope from print publishing into motion content.

What this work demonstrates

Long-form editorial design for publication

Designing for books and long-form publications is fundamentally different from campaign, deck, or interface work — it requires thinking about sustained reading experience across hundreds of pages, print production constraints, and the relationship between text density and visual breathing room. This project demonstrates that specific discipline at scale.

Cross-format system design

Building a single editorial system that held coherently across print books, digital course materials, and editorial publications — each with different aspect ratios, production requirements, and reading contexts — is a systems design challenge as much as a visual one. The ability to design a system flexible enough to adapt without losing its character is what makes it valuable at production scale.

Range — from editorial to motion

The Independence Day book project extended this engagement beyond print into a marketing video — demonstrating the ability to work across disciplines within a single project. For organisations looking for designers who can carry a content initiative from editorial concept through to motion delivery, this signals genuine range without the need for multiple specialists.
